Class TypeReferenceImp

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.JavaSourceElement
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.JavaProgramElement
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.JavaNonTerminalProgramElement
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.reference.TypeReferenceImp
All Implemented Interfaces:
ExpressionContainer, ModelElement, NamedModelElement, NamedProgramElement, NonTerminalProgramElement, ProgramElement, Reference, MemberReference, NameReference, PackageReferenceContainer, ReferencePrefix, ReferenceSuffix, TypeReference, TypeReferenceContainer, TypeReferenceInfix, SourceElement, EqualsModProperty<SourceElement>, SyntaxElement
Direct Known Subclasses:
SchemaTypeReference, TypeRef
public abstract class TypeReferenceImp extends JavaNonTerminalProgramElement implements TypeReference
TypeReferences reference Types by name. A TypeReference can refer to an outer or inner type and hence can also be a MemberReference, but does not have to. A TypeReference can also occur as part of a reference path and as a prefix for types, too. As a possible suffix for types, it can have other TypeReferences as a prefix, playing the role of a TypeReferenceContainer.

  • Constructor Details

    • TypeReferenceImp

      protected TypeReferenceImp(ExtList children, int dim)
      Constructor for the transformation of RECODER ASTs to KeY.
      Parameters:
      children - the children of this AST element as KeY classes. May contain: a ReferencePrefix (as prefix of the type reference) a ProgramElementName (as name for the type reference) Comments
      dim - the dimension of this type

    • TypeReferenceImp

  • Method Details

    • getFirstElement

      public SourceElement getFirstElement()
      Description copied from class: JavaSourceElement
      Finds the source element that occurs first in the source. The default implementation returns this element, which is correct for all terminal program elements, and many non terminals such as statements and prefixed operators.
      Specified by:
      getFirstElement in interface SourceElement
      Overrides:
      getFirstElement in class JavaSourceElement
      Returns:
      the first source element in the syntactical representation of this element, may be equals to this element.
      See Also:
      • JavaSourceElement.toSource()
      • JavaSourceElement.getStartPosition()

      Overrides:

      Returns:

    • match

      public MatchConditions match(SourceData source, MatchConditions matchCond)
      Description copied from interface: ProgramElement
      matches the source "text" (@link SourceData#getSource()) against the pattern represented by this object. In case of a successful match the resulting MatchConditions with the found instantiations of the schemavariables. If the match failed, null is returned instead.
      Specified by:
      match in interface ProgramElement
      Overrides:
      match in class JavaNonTerminalProgramElement
      Parameters:
      source - the SourceData with the program element to match
      matchCond - the MatchConditions found up to this point
      Returns:
      the resulting match conditions or null if the match failed
